pattern: Any cell strictly greater than its four orthogonal neighbours is acceptable, and binary-searching across one dimension by always stepping toward the larger neighbouring band guarantees converging on such a cell.
hints: A cell qualifies when it beats its up, down, left and right neighbours, with the outside of the grid treated as negative infinity.
Pick a middle column, find that column's largest value, and decide which side must contain a peak by comparing it to its horizontal neighbours.
Binary search over columns: the half holding the larger horizontal neighbour of the column maximum always contains a peak, so recurse there until one column remains.

edge_inputs.description: two equal-looking ridges but distinct values

stress.description: random grid with no two orthogonally adjacent equal values
